text: BREAKING: Second US aircraft loss in 24h — A-10 Warthog shot down during F-15E SAR ops in central Iran. Pilot ejected + rescued by SOF. Two HH-60 Black Hawks took small arms fire during extraction. F-15E WSO still missing. Iran offering bounty.
verificationNote: NPR, Air & Space Forces Magazine, Breaking Defense all confirmed.

text: Haaretz bombshell: Israeli public support for Iran war CRATERED to 45.5% from 63% at war start. Biggest single-poll swing since conflict began. Netanyahu losing domestic mandate. Combined with $45.8B defense budget strain — political pressure mounting.
verificationNote: Haaretz published full poll with methodology.

text: EU diplomatic push: Kallas-Wang Yi call on Iran. 19 new EU sanctions designations. Framework extended to April 2027. Meanwhile Italy's Meloni publicly breaks with Trump — first major NATO ally to refuse offensive operations. Traveling to Saudi Arabia for energy deals instead.
verificationNote: JURIST, The Spectator confirmed.

text: Oil update: Brent surged to $112.42 (+40.8% from pre-war) per Fortune. Physical dated Brent still at $141.36 — highest since 2008. WTI $111.54. Gas $4.09 (AAA). IEA chief: 'April will be much worse than March.' OPEC+ meets April 5. Trump Hormuz deadline April 6 — 2 days.
verificationNote: Fortune, CNBC, AAA confirmed prices.

text: FactCheck.org debunks multiple claims from Trump's April 1 primetime address: falsely claimed 45K killed in Iran protests (verified ~7K), falsely claimed nuclear sites 'totally obliterated' (intelligence: damaged/set back), falsely claimed $18T investment (WH site: $10.5T).
verificationNote: FactCheck.org, US News, Philadelphia Inquirer all fact-checked.

text: Day 35 EVE summary (7 PM PDT): 2nd US plane (A-10) downed; WSO missing; Brent $112.42; gas $4.09; Israeli support 45.5%; EU sanctions+Kallas-Wang Yi; Meloni breaks w/Trump; Trump April 6 Hormuz deadline 2 DAYS. OPEC+ April 5.
verificationNote: Compiled from NPR, Fortune, Haaretz, JURIST, Spectator.

text: Iranian missile barrage overnight: 15 impact sites across central Israel including Tel Aviv metro. Cluster munitions again — Neot Hovav industrial zone fire in Negev. Only 1 lightly wounded. Iron Dome gaps evident but casualty count remains remarkably low.
verificationNote: Haaretz, Times of Israel, JPost all confirmed impact locations and cluster munition use.

text: Major escalation on Iraq front: Islamic Resistance claims 19 attacks on US bases in a single day. al-Qaim PMF HQ and 34th Brigade Mosul both hit. This is roughly 3x the previous daily attack rate — Iraq proxy war intensifying rapidly.
verificationNote: ANI News and GlobalSecurity confirmed the 19-attack claim.

text: Iran rapidly repairing missile bunkers within hours per US intelligence. Using bulldozers and decoys. CNN: Iran retains ~50% of missile launchers and thousands of attack drones after 36 days. War 'near completion' narrative contradicted by own intelligence.
verificationNote: WION and CNN confirmed US intelligence assessment.

text: Iran Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf's Bab el-Mandeb post is significant: signals potential expansion of maritime blockade from Hormuz to Red Sea via Houthi allies. Would threaten ~12% of global trade. Second choke point = qualitatively different crisis.
verificationNote: Tribune India and Sunday Guardian confirmed Ghalibaf's X post.

text: UNSC Hormuz vote pushed to April 5. Bahrain resolution watered down to 'defensive measures' — but Russia, China, and France still likely to veto. UK convened 40-nation coalition separately (without US). Military planners meet next week on mine-clearing. Diplomatic paralysis.
verificationNote: France24 confirmed vote delay and veto expectations.

text: 3 Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers wounded (2 serious) at El Adeisse position in southern Lebanon. This follows 3 Indonesian peacekeepers killed earlier. Total UNIFIL casualties mounting — UN force increasingly caught in crossfire between IDF and Hezbollah.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera liveblog confirmed.

text: Day 36 AM summary (3:45 AM PDT): 15 Iran missile impacts in Israel (cluster munitions); Beirut Dahiyeh struck; 19 Iraqi militia attacks on US bases; Ghalibaf Bab el-Mandeb threat; Iran repairing bunkers; UNSC vote Apr 5; Pope ceasefire call; Brent $109.24; gas $4.09. April 6 deadline 2 DAYS.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNN, Al Jazeera, Haaretz, France24, Tribune India.

text: IAEA confirms projectile struck Bushehr Nuclear Plant perimeter — 1 security guard killed, auxiliary building damaged. No radiation increase. 4th attack on Bushehr since Feb 28. Grossi calls it crossing the 'reddest line.' This is the closest strike to a reactor since Zaporizhzhia.
verificationNote: IAEA official statement, Al Jazeera, NucNet confirmed.

text: IDF Navy targeted Ramada Hotel in Raouche, Beirut — 5 IRGC Quds Force commanders confirmed killed: Majid Hosseini, Ali Reza Biazar, Ahmad Rasouli, Hossein Ahmadlou, Abu Mohammad Ali. Massive blow to Iranian command structure in Lebanon theater.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, YNet confirmed 5 IRGC commanders killed.

text: Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Zone in Khuzestan struck by US-Israeli strikes. 6 plants hit: Fajr 1&2, Rejal, Amir Kabir, Abou Ali, Bandar-e Imam Khomeini. This is Iran's 2nd-largest petrochemical complex. Combined with 70% steel destruction — systematic economic targeting.
verificationNote: Tasnim News, The National, Arab News confirmed strikes.

text: Hezbollah has recaptured northern Khiam and re-entered Qantara in southern Lebanon. 48 IDF soldiers wounded in past 24h. This is the first significant Hezbollah territorial counterattack since IDF ground ops began. Division 162 being rushed in as reinforcement.
verificationNote: JPost confirmed Hezbollah counterattacks and IDF casualties.

text: Trump's 48-hour ultimatum now LIVE — April 6, 8 PM ET deadline. 'All Hell will reign down.' Iran rejected ceasefire, said response 'given on battlefield.' Vance-Ghalibaf talks via Pakistan stalled. Graham 'completely convinced' of overwhelming force. UNSC vote delayed to next week.
verificationNote: Axios, CBS News, Bloomberg confirmed Trump ultimatum.

text: Day 36 PM tracking: Iran opens Hormuz for 'essential goods' only (oil blockade stays). Oracle Dubai HQ damaged by drone/debris. 5 EU nations demand windfall tax. Russia condemns Bushehr. OPEC+ meeting tomorrow. Markets closed (Good Friday). Brent $109.03. April 6 deadline TOMORROW.
verificationNote: Xinhua, Euronews, TASS, CNBC confirmed.

text: CONFIRMED: Lebanon health ministry raises death toll to 1,422 killed, 3,935 injured since March 2. Deadliest week of the conflict. Multiple Tyre district strikes. Bekaa Valley increasingly isolated with 8+ bridges destroyed over Litani River.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera liveblog and Lebanese Health Ministry confirmed.

text: UPDATE: Pentagon says 365 US service members now wounded through Week 5. Missing F-15E WSO search enters Day 2 in Kohgiluyeh province. Iran IRGC claims to have recovered debris from both downed aircraft. Two SAR helicopters hit during rescue ops.
verificationNote: NBC News, The War Zone, CNN confirmed casualty update and search status.

text: OPEC+ ministerial meeting April 5 will be most consequential since alliance formation. Already agreed to 206K bbl/day increase for April. Further production decisions expected as Hormuz blockade enters Day 32. IEA considering SECOND strategic reserve release.
verificationNote: CNBC, Reuters, IEA statement confirmed.

text: Tel Aviv anti-war protest restricted to 150 people by IDF Home Front Command. Israeli Supreme Court overruled, ordered police to allow demonstrations. Growing domestic opposition — Haaretz: 'Israelis losing faith in the Iran war.'
verificationNote: Haaretz confirmed protest restrictions and Supreme Court ruling.

text: CRITICAL TIMELINE: Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum expires Monday April 6. Same day energy infrastructure strike pause expires. OPEC+ meets tomorrow. Pakistan mediation failed — Iran rejected US 15-point proposal as 'excessive, unrealistic.' Indirect talks via Vance-Ghalibaf stalled.
verificationNote: Axios, Washington Times, US News confirmed deadline and negotiation status.

text: Day 36 evening summary: F-15E airman missing Day 2. Lebanon 1,422 dead. Iraq 109 dead. 19 militia attacks on US bases in Iraq. Bushehr struck (4th time). Mahshahr petrochem 6 plants. Tel Aviv protests. OPEC+ tomorrow. April 6 deadline in 36 hours. Brent $109/WTI $112.
verificationNote: CNN, Al Jazeera, NBC, Axios liveblogs confirmed all items.
